---
title: "LRPLF01: Level factors for word reading (W)"

date: last-modified
date-format: "YYYY-MM-DD HH:mm:ss Z"

format:
  html:
    code-fold: true
    theme: cosmo
    number-sections: true
    toc: true
    toc-depth: 3
    code-links:
      - repo
---

::: {.callout-note}
Drafted by a LLM-based AI tool (Claude Code/Opus 4.8).
:::

::: {.callout-warning title="Warning: Preliminary model output"}

This model output is preliminary and likely to change as the model evolves and further data are received.

If the model was not fitted in "reporting" mode, the results will be more approximate than where additional sampling has been performed.

:::

```{python}
# | echo: false
import json
from pathlib import Path

import pandas as pd

_here = Path(".")
with open(_here / "config.json") as f:
    config = json.load(f)
factor_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "factor_summary.csv")
rope_summary = pd.read_csv(_here / "rope_summary.csv")
```

## Overview

LRPLF01 is a **level-factors** model for word reading (`ewrswr`): what is associated
with the word-reading score *level* at each of the four timepoints (t1–t4),
modelled on the logit scale with a Beta-Binomial likelihood and a child random
intercept. Unlike the gain model it is **not autoregressive** — there is no own
baseline; per-timepoint intercepts absorb the maturation trend.

::: {.callout-important title="Causal labelling + the level-model crossover caveat"}

Under the locked study DAG every non-randomised coefficient is an **adjusted
association** confounded by latent general ability (the child random intercept
repairs the time-invariant part) — "associated with", never "drives". **Crucially,
the group effect is modelled as `group × time` (a per-timepoint group coefficient
`b_grp_time[t]`, read as trajectory divergence) because after t2 the waitlist
crosses over: the group difference across the four timepoints is *not* a clean ITT
contrast.** The clean randomised contrast lives **only at t2** (`b_grp_time[1]`,
labelled *causal* in the table below); the t3/t4 group coefficients are
post-crossover and are cohort/timing associations, not treatment effects. SES is
excluded (non-DAG / redundant).

:::

**DAG factor set:** group $\times$ time · age (per timepoint) · cognitive ability
(block design) $\times$ time · group $\times$ ability · child random intercept.

## Model

$$
\operatorname{logit}\mu_{i,t} = \alpha + \alpha_{\text{time}[t]}
  + b_{\text{grp}[t]}\,\text{group}_i
  + \gamma_A A_{\text{std},t}
  + g_{\text{ability}[t]}\,z(\text{blocks})
  + \gamma_{\text{grp\_ability}}\,\text{group}_i\,z(\text{blocks})
  + u_{\text{child}[i]},
$$

with $W_t \sim \operatorname{BetaBinomial}(N, \mu\kappa, (1-\mu)\kappa)$, $N = 79$.
The per-timepoint group ($b_{\text{grp}[t]}$) and ability ($g_{\text{ability}[t]}$)
coefficients are the `group × time` and `ability × time` interactions; $t=1$ is t2,
the clean randomised contrast. Priors follow the shared template in `priors.py`.

![Model diagram](model_graph.svg){#fig-model .lightbox fig-align="left"}

## Priors

![alpha](prior_alpha.svg){width=24%}
![tau (group)](prior_tau.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_age](prior_gamma_age.svg){width=24%}
![gamma_cross (ability / interactions)](prior_gamma_cross.svg){width=24%}
![kappa](prior_kappa.svg){width=24%}

## Prior-predictive check

![Prior predictive](prior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Sampling diagnostics

```{python}
# | echo: false
diag = pd.read_csv(_here / "diagnostics.csv", index_col=0)
diag
```

![Trace](trace_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Energy](energy_plot.png){.lightbox}

![Posterior](posterior_plot.png){.lightbox}

## Posterior-predictive check

![Posterior predictive](posterior_predictive_check.png){.lightbox}

## Findings

### Factor summary

```{python}
# | echo: false
factor_summary
```

- `b_grp_time[t]` is the group effect at timepoint $t$ (0 = t1 … 3 = t4). Only
  `b_grp_time[1]` (t2) is the **clean randomised contrast** (labelled *causal*); the
  others are post-crossover cohort/timing associations.
- `g_ability_time[t]` (reported as `gamma_ability_time[t]`) is the ability
  association at each timepoint; `gamma_grp_ability` is the group $\times$ ability
  cross term. All non-t2 terms are adjusted associations under the DAG.
- `mean` / `lo` / `hi` = posterior mean and equal-tailed 95% credible interval
  (logit scale); `prob_positive` = $P(\text{coef} > 0)$.

### Reporting: the t2 contrast — direction, magnitude, practical significance

Following `notes/202606261304-evidence-strength-and-rope-reporting.md` and the
`METHODS.md` "Interpret" rule, the **one causal term** — the t2 randomised contrast
`b_grp_time[1]` — gets a ROPE-anchored items-scale report (the post-crossover
timepoints do **not**). Because group enters as a per-timepoint vector and carries a
group $\times$ ability interaction, the items-scale effect is the average marginal
effect of randomised assignment **at t2 only**, netting out both group terms at each
child's t2 profile (`reporting.level_t2_marginal_effect`). Magnitude is judged
against a minimally-important difference $\delta$ on the items scale
(`measures.ROPE_DELTA`, provisional, owned by the education lead). `tau_logit_*` is
the t2 contrast on the logit scale.

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
r = rope_summary.iloc[0]


from language_reading_predictors.statistical_models.reporting import odds_string as _odds

print(
    f"At t2 (the clean randomised contrast), the immediate arm's word reading was a "
    f"median of **{r.items_median:+.1f} items** above the wait-list "
    f"(50% CrI {r.items_lo50:+.1f} to {r.items_hi50:+.1f}; "
    f"95% CrI {r.items_lo:+.1f} to {r.items_hi:+.1f}). "
    f"**Direction** — evidence it helps: "
    f"pd = {r.pd:.3f} ({_odds(r.pd)}, *{r.direction_label} evidence*). "
    f"**Magnitude** — evidence the benefit is at least "
    f"$\\delta$ = {r.delta_items:g} items: "
    f"P = {r.prob_benefit_ge_delta:.3f} "
    f"({_odds(r.prob_benefit_ge_delta)}, *{r.benefit_label} evidence*); "
    f"probability practically negligible (inside the ROPE): {r.prob_in_rope:.3f}."
)
```

```{python}
# | echo: false
rope_summary.T
```

![ROPE-anchored posterior of the t2 randomised contrast, and the probability of a benefit of at least delta items](rope_summary.png){.lightbox}

::: {.callout-note title="Reading the evidence labels"}

The round-odds labels (*inconclusive / suggestive / moderate / strong / very
strong*) qualify the **evidence**, never the effect *size*, and are run separately
for the direction and the meaningful-benefit claims. At this study's sample size the
point estimate is, on average, magnitude-inflated (a Type-M / winner's-curse
effect), so lead with the interval, not the point. Only the t2 contrast is causal;
later-timepoint divergence is a post-crossover association.

:::

## LOO-PSIS

```{python}
# | echo: false
# | output: asis
print("```\n" + open(_here / "loo.txt").read() + "\n```")
```

## Configuration

```{python}
# | echo: false
config
```
